items.title: Switch bananarouter to share MongoDB with openaihk in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update changes the bananarouter deployment to use the shared `acedatacloud_openai` MongoDB database instead of its isolated one. As a result, tasks created by bananarouter are now visible through the public `POST /openai/tasks` API served by openaihk, resolving missing task visibility in user chat history. No code changes were made; only the deployment environment variable was updated. Future follow-ups include backfilling existing tasks and deprecating the old database.

items.title: Fix anonymous WeChat and AliPay payments redirect in Backend ⚡️
items.summary: This update ensures that anonymous users paying via WeChat or AliPay are redirected to the public `/orders/<id>` page instead of the authenticated `/console/orders/<id>` view. The `create_alipay_payment` function now accepts a `return_url` parameter, and `OrderViewSet.pay` sets `anon_return_url` for unauthenticated callers. Three new tests verify correct URL handling for anonymous payments. E2E tests confirm that payment flows now complete without losing context for anonymous users.

items.title: Confirmed No MCP Updates Needed for Overview Docs Sync in M C Ps 🚀
items.summary: This PR verifies that the `overview` documentation does not require any updates in the MCPs monorepo. A thorough scope check confirmed that there are no relevant packages or mappings for `overview`, resulting in no code or test file modifications. Consequently, the related issue can be closed as a no-op sync event.

items.title: Enable Anonymous Payments for Orders in Frontend Web 🎉
items.summary: This update introduces a new public payment route for orders, allowing users to pay without signing in. The `/orders/:id` route is now accessible to anonymous users, enhancing the payment experience by preventing unnecessary login redirects. Additionally, the WeChat payment dialog now copies the correct public URL, ensuring seamless access for recipients.

items.title: Feat(orders): allow unauthenticated pay for shared order URLs in Backend ✨
Feat(kong): canary route channel=br -> bananarouter for gpt-image-2 in Platform Deployment ✨
Introduce Bananarouter GPT-Image-2 Canary Upstream in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: Added a new submodule `bananarouter/` providing OpenAI-compatible `/images/generations` and `/images/edits` endpoints for the `gpt-image-2` model. The service includes routing, validation, error handling, and COS upload logic, isolated as a canary path via the `channel: br` header. Deployment manifests, Docker setup, and local development configs were included, enabling smooth opt-in without changing existing payloads. This allows traffic to shift to BananaRouter pods without affecting the default OpenAI upstream.

items.title: Enhance WeChat Payment Experience in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a mobile-friendly long-press QR code for WeChat payments when accessed through the WeChat in-app browser. The new dialog state features a single-column layout and removes irrelevant desktop tutorial images, improving usability for mobile users. Additionally, it includes internationalization support for long-press instructions across 18 locales.

items.title: Make WeChat Pay QR mobile-friendly with long-press tip in Frontend Web 🖥️
items.summary: This update introduces a dedicated mobile-in-WeChat payment dialog for the order page. The dialog now displays a single-column QR code with a long-press instruction, removing the desktop tutorial image and fitting 90% of the mobile width. All 12 locales received the new i18n key for the instruction. PC and mobile-outside-WeChat layouts remain unchanged.

items.title: Enable email, phone, and username login in WeChat browser in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update removes the `!isWechat` restrictions on email, phone, and username login forms in `Login.vue`, allowing these login methods to appear inside the WeChat browser. GitHub, Google OAuth, and Passkey providers remain restricted due to compatibility issues. The change preserves login behavior in regular browsers and ensures users without a bound WeChat account can log in seamlessly. All updates were verified with TypeScript checks, ESLint, and manual review.

items.title: Feat(sites): hydrate override edit dialog from un-rendered zh-cn source in Frontend Web ✨
Redact WeChat secrets from debug logs in Pay Backend 🔒
items.summary: This update prevents sensitive WeChat Pay credentials from being printed in debug logs on pod startup. The `WECHAT_MCH_PRIVATE_KEY_STRING` and `WECHAT_APIv3_KEY` now only report their presence and length, while non-secret identifiers continue to log normally. This closes a potential security exposure where logs could have been accessed by unauthorized users. No past log entries are altered; operators should rotate secrets and redeploy.

items.title: Auto-translate site override titles and summaries in Backend ⚡
items.summary: This PR promotes admin-entered `display_title` and `display_summary` on `SiteServiceOverride` and `SiteDocumentOverride` into the i18n pipeline, enabling site homepages to render in the visitor's locale instead of always showing the admin's Chinese input. It adds read-only serializer fields to surface the original Chinese for admin edits, updates views to sync translations on create/update/delete, and includes a new translation utility module with unit tests. The changes are fully compatible with existing data and integrate with the hourly translation propagation CronJob.

items.title: Streamline WeChat AppID Handling in Pay Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update refines the management of WeChat AppID by sourcing it directly from the `wechat` Secret, ensuring that the PayBackend's OA-credentials remain distinct from the `wechatpay` Secret. This change clarifies the responsibilities of each Secret and eliminates confusion regarding the dual AppID references. The modifications were made in both production and test deployment configurations.

items.title: Fix WeChat App Secret Source for Deployment in Pay Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update corrects the source of the `WECHAT_APP_SECRET` environment variable in the deployment configurations, switching it from the incorrect `wechatpay` Secret to the appropriate `wechat` Secret. This change resolves the `CreateContainerConfigError` that was preventing new pods from starting, ensuring that the JSAPI prepay functionality is live in production. No code changes were made, and the existing `wechatpay` Secret remains untouched for merchant credentials.

items.title: Enhance WeChat Payment Experience in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a three-state payment UI for WeChat, enhancing user experience across desktop and mobile platforms. Users can now seamlessly navigate payment options whether they are on a desktop browser, mobile browser, or within the WeChat app itself. Key changes include new UI components, improved localization support, and backend integration for a smoother payment process.
